stories.name: contact-selectable-predicate
stories.title: Contact-Selectable Predicate
stories.description: Add a new pure function to app/src/lib/contacts.ts that partitions listContacts(ownPubkeyHex, { includeArchived: true }) output into selectable / disabled-with-reason entries for a given group's memberPubkeys, mirroring eligibleGroupsForContact/addableGroupsForContact. Ship a colocated unit test covering the empty/all-selectable/all-disabled/case-insensitive/precedence/order-preservation cases.

includes: New pure predicate in app/src/lib/contacts.ts: (contacts: ContactListItem[], group: { memberPubkeys: string[] }) => partition entries { contact, selectable, disabledReason? }
already_member (case-insensitive memberPubkeys match) takes precedence over blocked (isArchived) per AC-ERR-1
Unit test file colocated with contacts.ts's existing predicate tests, mirroring app/tests/unit/contacts-groups.test.ts's shape
Keep app/src/lib/contacts.ts free of react / @nostr-dev-kit/ndk / app/src/context/** imports
excludes: Any change to InviteMemberModal.tsx or other UI (owned by S2)
Consumption of the predicate's output to decide guidance-state rendering (that assertion's subject is InviteMemberModal.tsx — see notes and AC-ERR-4 reassignment)

stories.notes: AC-ERR-4 is listed under this epic's 'S1' heading in both spec.md and acceptance-criteria.md, but its Observable subject is InviteMemberModal.tsx ('InviteMemberModal.tsx MUST use that all-disabled/empty condition ... to decide when to render the guidance state'), a file architecture.md reserves exclusively for S2 ('InviteMemberModal.tsx is the only file that may change UI/JSX for this epic'). A vitest test against contacts.ts alone cannot assert what the modal does at render time — that requires the Playwright/DOM surface AC-UX-6 already covers. Reassigned AC-ERR-4 to S2 (see S2.notes) rather than splitting story ownership across a UI file boundary. S1's contribution to AC-ERR-4 is structural only: its output shape must not require any additional/re-derived computation to detect the all-disabled/empty condition (a plain .every(e => !e.selectable) or .length === 0 check on the partition array suffices).

stories.name: invite-modal-contact-picker
stories.title: Invite Modal: Contact Picker + Guidance State
stories.description: Remove the npub free-text Input and QR-scan button from InviteMemberModal.tsx; add the invite-contact-select picker (built from S1's predicate) with disabled-option reason suffixes, and the guidance empty state with a /contacts link. Reshape submitInvite/resolveInviteTarget to accept a pubkey directly, update their DD-1/DD-8 doc-comments so they no longer claim to parse free text, update app/tests/unit/cards/inviteByCard.test.ts against the reshaped signatures, and add the new en/de i18n keys.

includes: Remove invite-npub-input and invite-scan-qr-btn (and their local state/wiring) from InviteMemberModal.tsx only — do not touch NpubQrButton/NpubQrModal/NpubQrScanner or their usage in settings.tsx/profile.tsx
Add invite-contact-select built from S1's predicate output, disabled <option>s carrying i18n-sourced reason suffixes distinct per already_member vs blocked

Add the guidance empty state (message + /contacts link) rendered when the predicate's output has zero selectable: true entries, using that condition directly (AC-ERR-4) with no separately re-derived check

Reshape submitInvite/resolveInviteTarget to accept a pubkey (derived via pubkeyToNpub from the selected contact's pubkeyHex) instead of parsing free text; keep them as a thin adapter (DD-11), do not touch contactCard.ts

Update the DD-1/DD-8 'single decode seam' doc-comments on resolveInviteTarget/submitInvite so they accurately attribute that seam to parseContactCard rather than implying these functions still parse free text
Update app/tests/unit/cards/inviteByCard.test.ts in the same change: remove/rewrite any of its 9 tests that exercise the now-unreachable free-text/card-parsing surface

Add new en/de i18n.ts keys: contact-picker label, already-member reason suffix, blocked reason suffix, guidance message, /contacts-link label; retitle inviteTitle; keep existing submit/success/error keys unchanged
Zero relay/NDK network calls on modal open / picker population (AC-SEC-1) — listContacts + the predicate are a pure local read
excludes: Any change to MarmotContext.inviteByNpub, key-package fetching, or the MLS commit (owned by 'MLS add-member' module, untouched this epic)
Any change to isAdmin gating or its MLS commit()-layer enforcement
Any change to contactCard.ts / parseContactCard itself

stories.notes: Consumes S1's ContactSelectabilityEntry seam (must land after S1). AC-ERR-4 reassigned here from S1's nominal heading in spec.md/acceptance-criteria.md: its Observable ('InviteMemberModal.tsx MUST use that all-disabled/empty condition ... to decide when to render the guidance state') is about this story's file, and its Manual Validation entry cross-references AC-UX-6, which this story also owns — verifying both together in one Playwright pass avoids a split assertion across two stories' verification.json files.

stories.name: e2e-shared-contact-seeding-helper
stories.title: E2E Infra: Shared Contact-Seeding + Picker Helper
stories.description: Add one shared 'ensure contact via the production add-contact path, then invite via invite-contact-select' helper to group-setup.ts, replacing createGroupAndInvite's direct invite-npub-input fill. Update the 25 directly-affected specs (including the 11 transitive group-setup.ts importers) plus the two bespoke specs (groups-error-cases, groups-member-profiles) so no e2e file references invite-npub-input, and add new coverage specs for the guidance state and pending-contact-selectable behavior.

includes: One shared helper in app/tests/e2e/helpers/group-setup.ts: page.goto('/add#c=' + npub) on the inviter's page, then drive invite-contact-select + invite-submit-btn
Replace createGroupAndInvite's direct invite-npub-input fill with the new helper
Update all specs/importers so zero files under app/tests/e2e/ contain 'invite-npub-input' (AC-E2E-2)

groups-error-cases.spec.ts: seed the unpublished-KeyPackage contact before selecting it (AC-E2E-4); delete 'Invalid npub format shows error' and replace with a disabled-option-cannot-be-selected test in the same commit (AC-E2E-5)
groups-member-profiles.spec.ts: seed the invitee as a contact before driving the picker in both affected tests (AC-E2E-6)
New spec: guidance state end-to-end for a user with zero selectable contacts (AC-E2E-7)
New spec: pending-contact-is-already-member (disabled, no re-invite path) (AC-E2E-8, corrected post-implementation)
excludes: Any production code change (contacts.ts, InviteMemberModal.tsx, i18n.ts are frozen inputs to this story)
Ad-hoc per-spec seeding logic outside the shared helper

stories.notes: Drives the real invite-contact-select picker shipped by S2 (must land after S2) — no mock of the modal is used or needed since implementation is sequential, not parallel.

dependency_notes: Strict linear chain S1 -> S2 -> S3, matching the spec's own sketch: S2 consumes the ContactSelectabilityEntry seam S1 creates (the predicate must exist before the modal can be reshaped to use it), and S3's e2e helper drives the invite-contact-select picker S2 builds (there is nothing to seed-and-select until the picker exists). No parallel implementation is intended or needed. One AC reassignment from the spec's story sketch: AC-ERR-4 moved from S1 to S2 because its Observable clause governs InviteMemberModal.tsx (S2's owning_module, and the only file architecture.md permits for UI/JSX changes this epic), not contacts.ts — see S1.notes and S2.notes for the full rationale.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks are introduced. Stories are implemented sequentially (S1 before S2 before S3), so each downstream story consumes the real upstream artifact rather than a stand-in.

seams.data: Per-contact selectability partition for a group's contact-invite picker

source.artifact: app/src/lib/contacts.ts (new 'selectable contacts for a group' predicate)

invariants: Output array has exactly one entry per input ContactListItem, in the same order as the input array (order-preservation, AC-STRUCT-1 / AC-TEST-1f).
disabledReason is present if and only if selectable is false.

A contact whose pubkeyHex matches, case-insensitively, an entry in group.memberPubkeys is always { selectable: false, disabledReason: 'already_member' }, regardless of isArchived — already-member precedence over blocked (AC-ERR-1, AC-TEST-1e).
A contact not matching memberPubkeys with isArchived === true is { selectable: false, disabledReason: 'blocked' } (AC-ERR-2).
A contact satisfying neither condition is { selectable: true, disabledReason: undefined } (AC-ERR-3).

The all-disabled/empty condition (every entry selectable: false, including the empty-input case) is detectable directly from the output array with no additional re-derived check, since AC-ERR-4 requires the consuming modal to use exactly this condition.
